role_title: Team Analyst – Global Focused Growth Strategy

location.office_time_required: up to one day per week from home (predominantly in-office)

secondary_archetype: Buy-side Investment Analyst / Equity Research Analyst

level_signals: Bachelor's degree and 4+ years of relevant buy-side/sell-side investment experience
Described as 'apprenticeship with senior investors' suggesting non-senior but experienced hire
Responsibilities expand 'if appropriate' implying growth role

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree and 4+ years of relevant buy-side/sell-side investment experience.
Advanced Excel and financial modeling capability, including three-statement builds and time-series analytics.
Strong academic foundation combining liberal-arts reasoning with quantitative training (e.g., calculus, statistics, econometrics; CFA coursework).
Working knowledge of equity markets and instruments.
Proven facility with AI tools and prompt engineering for research use cases.
Comfort operating in a high-intensity, performance-oriented team environment.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Self-directed, confident contributor who works independently and collaborates effectively.
Demonstrated passion for equity investing and market discovery.
Track record of converting deep research into clear, actionable insights.

explicitly_excluded: FINRA licenses are not required and will not be supported for this role.

scope.surface: Global Focused Growth Strategy — diversified global growth equity mandate managed by Portfolio Manager Dave Eiswert
scope.business_model: active equity investment management (buy-side asset manager)
primary_metrics_inferred: Investment insight generation and accuracy of forecasts
Research speed and quality improvements via AI workflows
core_responsibilities: Adopt, execute, and continuously improve the Global Focused Growth investment framework (historical analysis, insight development, and forward modeling).
Lead scoped company/industry research projects and thematic workstreams aligned to portfolio needs.
Build and maintain three-statement and scenario models; synthesize market and alternative data into forecasts.
Partner with the Portfolio Manager and senior analysts to prepare briefs, update views, and translate research into portfolio actions.
Engage CEOs/CFOs and other external experts through meetings, calls, and road visits to test and refine theses.
Extremely detail oriented documentation, compliance discipline, and data hygiene; contribute to reusable templates and best practices.
Track ex-ante assumptions versus ex-post outcomes and participate in performance attribution reviews.
Conduct primary diligence (filings, transcripts, channel checks, site visits) to deepen company and industry understanding.
Develop investment recommendations over time and accept outcome accountability as responsibilities expand if appropriate.
Design and apply AI-driven workflows (prompt libraries, retrieval, automation) to improve research speed and quality.

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated and highly competitive ($180K–$300K base)
Clear reporting structure to named Portfolio Manager (Dave Eiswert)
Explicit mention of AI tool usage as a core capability, signaling modern research culture
Apprenticeship model with senior investors for long-term career development
Annual bonus eligibility and generous retirement plan mentioned
Role scope and responsibilities are detailed and specific
red_flags: Hybrid policy allows only up to one day per week from home — heavily in-office requirement
Role is listed in Baltimore, MD but salary ranges also list remote workers; remote eligibility is ambiguous
Very wide compensation band ($180K–$300K) makes it difficult to gauge where a candidate would land
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
Role is not a traditional PM role; archetype is investment analyst, which may attract different audiences

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and compensation range)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as global asset manager)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from salary range listing 'remote workers' as a salary tier)
ambiguities: The job URL includes 'Baltimore-MD' but the compensation section lists salary ranges for Maryland, Colorado, Washington, D.C., New York, California, and 'remote workers' — it is unclear whether this role can be fully remote or if 'remote workers' refers to an existing category. Treated as hybrid with remote_us_eligible: true given the explicit 'remote workers' salary tier.
The hybrid policy states 'up to one day per week from home' which implies predominantly in-office (~4 days/week), yet remote pay tiers are listed — possible inconsistency.
location.pay_differential: all three listed salary tiers show the same $180K–$300K range, so no differential was applied, though multiple geographies are listed.
